module.name: funcplot
module.description: Plot y = f(x) curves (SVG): one JavaScript-style math expression per section, e.g. "sin(x)" or "x^2 - 2 | label". Auto-colored, with axes and grid.
module.aiSpec:
A FUNCTION PLOT: draws one or more y = f(x) curves over an x-range.
Body: split the sections with `<!-- @ -->`, one function per section written `expression | label` (label optional). Expressions use x and standard math functions: `sin cos tan exp log sqrt abs floor PI E`, with operators `+ - * / ^` (e.g. `x^2 - 2`, `sin(x)*exp(-x/5)`). Only math characters are allowed; anything else is ignored. Curves are auto-coloured and labels appear in a small legend.
When NOT to use: to plot real data points use @chartjs; for a Gaussian bell curve use @distribution.
Parameters: - `xmin` / `xmax` : x-range. Defaults -5 and 5. Any real numbers. - `ymin` / `ymax` : y-range. Blank = auto-fit from the samples. Any real numbers. - `grid` : true/false — show the background grid. Default true. - `axis` : true/false — draw the x=0 / y=0 axis lines when 0 is in range. Default true. - `legend` : true/false — show the label legend. Default true. - `height` : plot height in SVG units (changes the aspect ratio; width is 160). Default 100. Range 40–240. - `strokewidth` : curve line thickness. Default 1.3. Range 0.3–5.
<!-- @funcplot xmin: -6.28, xmax: 6.28, height: 120, strokewidth: 2 --> sin(x) | sine <!-- @ --> cos(x) | cosine <!-- @end -->

param.name: xmin
param.label: x min
param.desc: Left edge of the x-range; any real number.

param.name: xmax
param.label: x max
param.desc: Right edge of the x-range; any real number.

param.name: ymin
param.label: y min
param.desc: Bottom of the y-range; blank = auto-fit.

param.name: ymax
param.label: y max
param.desc: Top of the y-range; blank = auto-fit.

param.name: axis
param.label: Show axes
param.desc: Draw x=0 / y=0 axis lines when 0 is in range.

param.name: height
param.label: Height
param.desc: Plot height in SVG units (width is 160); changes aspect ratio.

snippet.label: Function plot y=f(x)
snippet.text: <!-- @funcplot xmin: -6.28, xmax: 6.28 --> sin(x) | sin <!-- @ --> cos(x) | cos <!-- @end -->
snippet.description: Curves from math expressions in x; one per section, "expr | label".
